mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as tightening works through financial conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been on a sustained rising trajectory into March 2023
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 60-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns are positive in >55% of rising-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection from labor market tightness to deterioration, historically preceding equity drawdowns as markets price higher recession probability over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following a triggered UNRATE rise are positive in more than 55% of historical trigger instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates simultaneous weakening in consumer spending and residential investment, the two largest cyclical demand components, and jointly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter that neither series predicts alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS momentum x HOUST momentum) fails to add explanatory power over either single-series regression, measured by no improvement in out-of-sample Rank IC beyond 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO signals expanding industrial output, which supports forward earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, but only during early-cycle expansions when capacity utilization is still rising rather than late-cycle overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.739 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) when restricted to periods independently classified as early-cycle expansion.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises faster than CPIAUCSL inflation, the implied real policy rate climbs sharply, tightening financial conditions abruptly and triggering short-horizon equity selloffs as markets reprice risk premia within days of the divergence widening.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-minus-CPIAUCSL-growth real rate proxy shows no significant negative correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across the tightening cycle sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor demand and income generation that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146730 thousand as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in HOUST exceeding 10% signals a housing sector contraction that historically leads broader economic slowdowns by two to three quarters, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the effect propagates to construction, durable goods, and credit markets.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1155 thousand units as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Following a triggered 10% YoY HOUST decline, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% during a period of acute financial-sector stress (as occurred around the March 2023 regional bank failures), tightened credit conditions combine with flight-to-quality dynamics to produce sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the immediate following week before any policy response can stabilize markets.
evidence_refs: fact:regional banking stress event occurring in March 2023 decision window
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with financial stability stress
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following FEDFUNDS crossing 4.5% during identified stress windows are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across such episodes.
